q.en: Where is the Great Mosque of Djenné located?
q.zh: 杰内大清真寺位于哪里？

options.zh: 非洲马里共和国的杰内

options.en: Mecca, Saudi Arabia according to some versions of the story
options.zh: 沙特阿拉伯麦加（按书中另一种说法）

explain.en: The mosque is in the town of Djenné, in the West African country of Mali.
explain.zh: 该清真寺位于西非马里共和国的杰内镇。

options.en: Markets for trading gold according to some versions of the story
options.zh: 买卖黄金的市场（按书中另一种说法）

explain.en: Mosques are places of worship for Muslims, who follow the religion of Islam.
explain.zh: 清真寺是信仰伊斯兰教的穆斯林的礼拜场所。

q.en: What is the Great Mosque of Djenné mainly made of?
q.zh: 杰内大清真寺主要由什么建成？

options.en: Stone from Egypt according to some versions of the story
options.zh: 来自埃及的石头（按书中另一种说法）

explain.en: It is built from sun-baked mud bricks, mortar, and plaster—the largest mud-brick building in the world.
explain.zh: 它由日晒泥砖、泥灰浆和泥灰泥建成，是世界上最大的泥砖建筑。

options.en: Bundles of palm wood sticks that jut from the walls
options.zh: 从墙里伸出的成束棕榈木条

options.en: A musical instrument according to some versions of the story
options.zh: 一种乐器（按书中另一种说法）

explain.en: Toron are palm-wood sticks jutting from the walls; they support the walls and serve as scaffolding.
explain.zh: toron是从墙中伸出的棕榈木条，既支撑墙体，又充当修缮时的脚手架。

q.en: In what year was today's mosque completed?
q.zh: 今天这座清真寺是哪一年建成的？

explain.en: The mosque we see today was completed in 1907 on the site of the original.
explain.zh: 我们今天见到的清真寺于1907年在原址建成。

q.en: What tops each of the mosque's three minarets?
q.zh: 清真寺三座宣礼塔的顶端各有什么？

options.en: An ostrich egg (symbol of purity and fertility)
options.zh: 一枚鸵鸟蛋（纯洁与多产的象征）

options.en: A weather vane according to some versions of the story
options.zh: 一个风向标（按书中另一种说法）

explain.en: Each minaret is topped with an ornate ostrich egg symbolizing purity and fertility.
explain.zh: 每座宣礼塔顶端都有一枚精美的鸵鸟蛋，象征纯洁与多产。

q.en: What is the annual festival to replaster the mosque called?
q.zh: 每年为清真寺重新抹泥的节庆叫什么？

options.en: Carnival according to some versions of the story
options.zh: 狂欢节（按书中另一种说法）

explain.en: Each year the community holds the Crépissage de la Grande Mosquée to apply fresh mud plaster.
explain.zh: 每年全镇举行“大清真寺抹泥节”来重新涂抹泥灰。

q.en: Why was the mosque built on a raised platform?
q.zh: 清真寺为什么建在高出地面的平台上？

options.en: To make it taller than the trees according to some versions of the story
options.zh: 为了比树更高（按书中另一种说法）

options.en: To avoid flooding from the nearby Bani River
options.zh: 为了避免附近巴尼河泛滥被淹

explain.en: It sits on a 3-meter raised platform to avoid flooding from the Bani River.
explain.zh: 它建在3米高的平台上，以避开巴尼河的洪水。

q.en: In what year was Djenné named a World Heritage Site?
q.zh: 杰内是在哪一年被列为世界遗产的？

options.en: 2000 according to some versions of the story

explain.en: Djenné was named a World Heritage Site in 1988, which protects its history and explains why locals preserve rather than modernize it.
explain.zh: 杰内于1988年被列为世界遗产，这保护了其历史，也解释了当地人选择保护而非改造它的原因。

q.en: Which direction does the prayer (qibla) wall face?

explain.en: The qibla wall faces east toward Mecca, the birthplace of Islam.
explain.zh: 礼拜墙朝东，面向伊斯兰教的发源地麦加。

prompt.en: The whole community of Djenné works together each year to replaster their mud mosque, and they have chosen to preserve it rather than modernize it. Why might a community value preserving an old building over updating it, and what old place in your area do you think is worth preserving?
prompt.zh: 杰内全镇每年共同为泥清真寺重新抹泥，并且选择保护它而不是现代化改造。一个社群为何会看重保护老建筑而非翻新它？你所在的地区有什么老地方值得保护？

en: Give one reason a community might preserve an old building (culture, history, identity).
Name a real or imagined old place you value and say why it matters.
zh: 写出一个社群保护老建筑的理由（文化、历史、身份认同等）。
说出一处你珍视的真实或想象中的老地方，并说明它为何重要。
